grambank_features.name: What is the order of numeral and noun in the NP?
grambank_features.gloss: Order of numeral and noun in the noun phrase: 1=Numeral-Noun, 2=Noun-Numeral, 3=both orders occur.

grambank_features.name: Can polar interrogation be marked by intonation only?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether yes/no questions can be marked by intonation alone.

grambank_features.name: Can polar interrogation be indicated by a special word order?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether yes/no questions can be marked by a special word order.

grambank_features.name: Do (nominal) content interrogatives normally or frequently occur in situ?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether content interrogatives normally stay in situ (not fronted).

grambank_features.name: Can the relative clause follow the noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether a relative clause can follow its head noun.

grambank_features.name: Can the relative clause precede the noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether a relative clause can precede its head noun.

grambank_features.name: Are there internally-headed relative clauses?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there are internally-headed relative clauses.

grambank_features.name: Are there three or more distance contrasts in demonstratives?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether demonstratives make three or more distance contrasts (e.g. near/mid/far).

grambank_features.name: Are there definite or specific articles?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has a definite or specific article (a grammatical word like English 'the').

grambank_features.name: Do indefinite nominals commonly have indefinite articles?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether indefinite nominals commonly take an indefinite article (like English 'a/an').

grambank_features.name: What is the order of adnominal demonstrative and noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Order of an adnominal demonstrative ('this/that') and noun: 1=Dem-Noun, 2=Noun-Dem, 3=both.

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender distinction in independent 3rd person pronouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether independent 3rd-person pronouns mark a gender distinction (he vs she).

grambank_features.name: Is there a productive morphological pattern for deriving an action/state noun from a verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an action/state noun can be productively derived from a verb (e.g. 'run' -> 'running').

grambank_features.name: Is there a productive morphological pattern for deriving an agent noun from a verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an agent noun can be productively derived from a verb (e.g. 'teach' -> 'teacher').

grambank_features.name: Is there a productive morphological pattern for deriving an object noun from a verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an object/result noun can be productively derived from a verb.

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender/noun class system where shape is a factor in class assignment?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the gender/noun-class system uses shape as a class-assignment factor.

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender/noun class system where animacy is a factor in class assignment?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the gender/noun-class system uses animacy as a class-assignment factor.

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender/noun class system where plant status is a factor in class assignment?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the gender/noun-class system uses plant status as a class-assignment factor.

grambank_features.name: Are there numeral classifiers?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has numeral classifiers (a sortal element required when counting).

grambank_features.name: Are there possessive classifiers?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has possessive classifiers.

grambank_features.name: What is the pragmatically unmarked order of adnominal possessor noun and possessed noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Unmarked order of possessor noun and possessed noun: 1=Possessor-Possessed, 2=Possessed-Possessor, 3=both.

grambank_features.name: Can an adnominal property word agree with the noun in gender/noun class?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an adjective-like property word can agree with its noun in gender/noun class.

grambank_features.name: Can an adnominal demonstrative agree with the noun in gender/noun class?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an adnominal demonstrative can agree with its noun in gender/noun class.

grambank_features.name: Can an article agree with the noun in gender/noun class?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an article can agree with its noun in gender/noun class.

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender system where a noun's phonological properties are a factor in class assignment?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether a noun's phonological shape is a factor in gender/class assignment.

grambank_features.name: What is the order of adnominal property word and noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Order of adjective-like property word and noun: 0=not used attributively, 1=Adj-N, 2=N-Adj, 3=both.

grambank_features.name: Can standard negation be marked by a non-inflecting word ("auxiliary particle")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether standard negation can be marked by a non-inflecting negative particle.

grambank_features.name: Is plural number regularly marked in the noun phrase by a dedicated phonologically free element?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether plural number is marked in the NP by a dedicated free (unbound) element.

grambank_features.name: Can adnominal possession be marked by a prefix on the possessor?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether adnominal possession can be marked by a prefix on the possessor.

grambank_features.name: Can adnominal possession be marked by a prefix on the possessed noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether adnominal possession can be marked by a prefix on the possessed noun.

grambank_features.name: Can adnominal possession be marked by a suffix on the possessor?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether adnominal possession can be marked by a suffix on the possessor.

grambank_features.name: Can adnominal possession be marked by a suffix on the possessed noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether adnominal possession can be marked by a suffix on the possessed noun.

grambank_features.name: Is there a decimal numeral system?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the numeral system is decimal (base 10).

grambank_features.name: Is there synchronic evidence for any element of a quinary numeral system?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is any synchronic evidence of a quinary (base 5) element.

grambank_features.name: Are nominal conjunction and comitative expressed by different elements?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether noun-conjoining ('X and Y') and comitative ('X with Y') use different markers.

grambank_features.name: Are there several nouns (more than three) which are suppletive for number?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether more than three nouns are suppletive for number (a different root for singular vs plural).

grambank_features.name: Is there productive overt morphological singular marking on nouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is productive overt singular marking on nouns.

grambank_features.name: Are there prepositions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has prepositions.

grambank_features.name: Is there grammatical marking of direct evidence (perceived with the senses)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is grammatical marking of direct (sensory) evidence.

grambank_features.name: Is there grammatical marking of indirect evidence (hearsay, inference, etc.)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is grammatical marking of indirect evidence (hearsay, inference).

grambank_features.name: Is there any ergative alignment of flagging?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether any part of the case/adposition (flagging) system shows ergative alignment.

grambank_features.name: Is there a politeness distinction in 2nd person forms?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a politeness (T/V) distinction in 2nd-person forms.

grambank_features.name: Is there a distinction between inclusive and exclusive?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the pronoun system distinguishes inclusive ('we incl. you') from exclusive ('we excl. you').

grambank_features.name: Is there a phonologically independent reflexive pronoun?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a phonologically independent reflexive pronoun.

grambank_features.name: Do core adjectives (defined semantically as property concepts such as value, shape, age, dimension) act like verbs in predicative position?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether core adjectives behave like verbs when used as a predicate ('the dog is big' patterns like 'the dog runs').

grambank_features.name: Can the S argument be indexed by a suffix/enclitic on the verb in the simple main clause?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the S (intransitive subject) can be indexed by a suffix/enclitic on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Is there a benefactive applicative marker on the verb (including indexing)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a benefactive applicative marker on the verb ('do X for someone').

grambank_features.name: Is there an instrumental applicative marker on the verb (including indexing)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is an instrumental applicative marker on the verb ('do X with an instrument').

grambank_features.name: Can standard negation be marked by an affix, clitic or modification of the verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether standard negation can be marked by an affix, clitic, or verb modification.

grambank_features.name: Is there verb suppletion for participant number?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether verbs are suppletive for participant number (different root for singular vs plural participant).

grambank_features.name: Is there verb suppletion for tense or aspect?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether verbs are suppletive for tense or aspect.

grambank_features.name: Is there a copula for predicate nominals?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a copula for predicate nominals ('X is a Y').

grambank_features.name: Are there serial verb constructions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there are serial verb constructions (two+ verbs in one clause without a linker).

grambank_features.name: Can mood be marked by an inflecting word ("auxiliary verb")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether mood can be marked by an inflecting auxiliary verb.

grambank_features.name: Can aspect be marked by an inflecting word ("auxiliary verb")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether aspect can be marked by an inflecting auxiliary verb.

grambank_features.name: Can tense be marked by an inflecting word ("auxiliary verb")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether tense can be marked by an inflecting auxiliary verb.

grambank_features.name: Is verb compounding a regular process?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether verb compounding is a regular process.

grambank_features.name: Are there verb-adjunct (aka light-verb) constructions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there are verb-adjunct (light-verb / coverb) constructions.

grambank_features.name: Is incorporation of nouns into verbs a productive intransitivizing process?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether noun incorporation into the verb is a productive intransitivizing process.

grambank_features.name: Is a pragmatically unmarked constituent order verb-initial for transitive clauses?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the unmarked transitive constituent order is verb-initial.

grambank_features.name: Is the order of core argument (i.e. S/A/P) constituents fixed?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the order of core (S/A/P) constituents is fixed.

grambank_features.name: Is there a difference between imperative (prohibitive) and declarative negation constructions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether prohibitive (negative imperative) negation differs from declarative negation.

grambank_features.name: Is there a morphological passive marked on the lexical verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a morphological passive marked on the lexical verb.

grambank_features.name: Is there a morphological antipassive marked on the lexical verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a morphological antipassive marked on the lexical verb.

grambank_features.name: Are causatives formed by affixes or clitics on verbs?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether causatives are formed by affixes/clitics on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Can polar interrogation be indicated by overt verbal morphology only?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether yes/no questions can be marked by verbal morphology alone.

grambank_features.name: Can standard negation be marked by an inflecting word ("auxiliary verb")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether standard negation can be marked by an inflecting negative auxiliary verb.

grambank_features.name: Are there multiple past or multiple future tenses, distinguishing distance from Time of Reference?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there are multiple past or future tenses distinguishing distance in time (e.g. recent vs remote past).

aus_extension.name: Distance contrasts in demonstratives
aus_extension.gloss: Number of distance contrasts (2, 3+ deictic terms).

aus_extension.name: Multiple (distance) past/future tenses
aus_extension.gloss: Whether there are multiple past or future tenses distinguishing temporal distance (e.g. recent vs remote past) — common in Australian languages.

aus_extension.name: Verb suppletion for participant number
aus_extension.gloss: Whether a verb uses a different root for singular vs plural participant (e.g. 'go.sg' vs 'go.pl').

aus_extension.name: Morphological antipassive
aus_extension.gloss: A morphological antipassive on the lexical verb (demotes P; salient in ergative languages).

aus_extension.name: Causative by affix/clitic
aus_extension.gloss: Whether causatives are formed by an affix/clitic on the verb.

aus_extension.name: Prohibitive vs declarative negation
aus_extension.gloss: Whether the prohibitive (negative imperative) uses a different construction from declarative negation.

aus_extension.name: Adposition type
aus_extension.gloss: Prepositions vs postpositions vs no dominant adpositions.

aus_extension.name: Numeral base system
aus_extension.gloss: Base of the numeral system: decimal, quinary, vigesimal, body-part tally, or restricted (many Australian languages have very small numeral systems).

aus_extension.name: Verb–adjunct (coverb / light-verb) constructions
aus_extension.gloss: Whether predicates are built from an uninflecting coverb + a small closed class of inflecting light verbs (widespread in non-Pama-Nyungan Australia).

aus_extension.name: Noun incorporation (intransitivizing)
aus_extension.gloss: Whether noun incorporation into the verb is a productive intransitivizing process.
